[Layer 4] The contract's struct definition has copy or drop ability, which can be misused to duplicate or silently discard value or obligation objects.
findings.recommendation: Hot-potato structs must have no abilities and a matched consume function.
findings.patch_before: struct Receipt has copy, drop { amount: u64 }

findings.description: [Layer 4] The contract's public function writes to shared state without a capability parameter, allowing anyone to mutate critical state.
findings.recommendation: Gate privileged functions with a typed capability argument instead of address checks.
findings.patch_before:
public fun withdraw(vault: &mut Vault, amount: u64,
    ctx: &mut TxContext) { ... }
findings.patch_after:
public fun withdraw(_cap: &AdminCap, vault: &mut Vault,
    amount: u64, ctx: &mut TxContext) { ... }

findings.description: Third-party library imported without pinned version in Move.lock — Cetus integer-mate class.
findings.recommendation: Audit math library dependencies line-by-line. Pin versions in Move.lock. Fuzz at boundary values.

findings.description: Function accepts Coin<T> without validating exact payment or returning change.
findings.recommendation: Assert exact payment `coin::value(&p) == price` OR split and return change to sender.

findings.description: Division/modulo with no preceding assert(denominator != 0).
findings.recommendation: Add `assert!(denominator != 0, EDivisionByZero);` before every dynamic division.

findings.description: Unbounded loop over user-controlled collection — gas cost grows without bound, eventual DoS.
findings.recommendation: Cap collection size: `assert!(vector::length(&col) < MAX_SIZE, EFull)`. Process in batches.

findings.description: First tuple element (status/bool) discarded with _ — error code swallowed.
findings.recommendation: Never discard first element of tuple from arithmetic/financial helpers; assert ok flag.

findings.description: [Layer 4] The contract's function returns a struct without a matching consume or repay function in the same module, which can lead to silently discarded obligations.
findings.recommendation: Never discard Option<T> or error results with let _ = .... Use option::destroy_some or propagate errors.

findings.description: [Layer 4] The contract's division operator has a denominator that can be zero, which can lead to a division-by-zero error.
findings.recommendation: Use checked_shl instead of raw bit-shifts. The Cetus exploit lost $223M to this pattern.

findings.description: Swap/liquidity function missing pool-pause state check — trading continues during emergency halt.
findings.recommendation: Assert at start of trade functions: `assert!(!pool.paused, EPoolPaused);`.

findings.description: [Layer 4] The contract's loop iterates over a user-controlled collection without a length cap, which can lead to a denial-of-service attack.
findings.recommendation: Bound all loops with explicit iteration limits. Avoid on-chain unbounded recursion.
